## Epic 1 — Core Mechanics (#12)

**Dependency:** #10
**Est. Effort:** Medium

### Goal
Design the core 3d6 resolution mechanic — the foundation every other system in the game builds on.

### Design Requirements
- **Roll:** 3d6 + Attribute Modifier + Skill Bonus + Game Modifiers
- **Weak:** 1-6 — partial success or success with complication
- **Standard:** 7-12 — full success, expected outcome
- **Strong:** 13-18+ — exceptional success, bonus effect
- **Critical:** Three natural 6s — automatic strong + special critical effect
- **Fumble:** Three natural 1s — automatic failure + complication

### Open Questions to Resolve
- Are tier boundaries fixed (1-6, 7-12, 13-18+) or do they shift with difficulty?
- How does difficulty work? Negative modifiers to the roll? Higher tier thresholds?
- What's the baseline difficulty for "standard" tasks?
- How do opposed rolls work (e.g., stealth vs perception)?
- Can you "take 10" or automatically succeed on trivial tasks?
- What happens on a tie in opposed rolls?

### Tasks
- [ ] Document the full resolution procedure (step by step)
- [ ] Define Weak/Standard/Strong outcomes for generic tasks
- [ ] Define critical hit bonus effects
- [ ] Define fumble complication table or guidelines
- [ ] Define opposed roll rules
- [ ] Define difficulty modifiers and when to apply them
- [ ] Define "always hit" philosophy — how does it change the game flow?
- [ ] Write chapter 06 — Core Resolution Mechanics as AsciiDoc
- [ ] Include 3+ worked examples (combat, skill check, social, magic)
- [ ] Cross-reference to magic (ch10), combat (ch13), skills (ch07)
